sprint: Sprint 28 -- V0.27.2 Bug Fixes (drive_summary + sync_history + 4 hygiene)
backlogItem: Bug-fix sprint surfaced by V0.27.1's success unblocking engine-on data capture (Drive 6 + Drive 7 IRL validation 2026-05-08). Six regressions / data-hygiene gaps in metadata / sync / drain / observability layers. F-005 + F-007 are load-bearing P1 fixes; the four P3 stories close drain-event / startup_log / battery_health_log / statistics-tagging hygiene gaps that hid behind the 'no data ever' symptom from Sprint 25-27.

testBaseline.note: Post-V0.27.1 baseline (V0.27.1 main@cfdb2d9). Rex Session 180 reported fastSuite=4144 GREEN post-V0.27.1; verify exact count at sprint start.

sprintNotes: GOAL: Close the six regressions surfaced when V0.27.1 unblocked engine-on data capture. Drives 6+7 + Drain 8 in-vehicle on 2026-05-08 validated F-002/F-003/F-004/F-006/F-008/F-009/F-010/F-011/F-012 but exposed F-005 (drive_summary INSERT not firing) and F-007 (sync_history table missing on Pi) as broken, plus four data-hygiene gaps. V0.27.2 fixes all six with real engine-on validation gating the merge to main.

EXECUTION ORDER (deps-aware): TIER 1 (P1, parallel) -- US-304 (drive_summary writer regression) + US-305 (sync_history v0007 migration gap). TIER 2 (P3, parallel, independent) -- US-306 (statistics drive_id NULL; likely same wiring fix family as US-304 but no hard dep) + US-307 (drain_event close-before-poweroff) + US-308 (startup_log graceful-shutdown detection) + US-309 (battery_health_log legacy column cleanup).

THEME: 'V0.27.1 unmasked these.' Bugs surfaced because the primary mission (engine-on telemetry) finally works again and exercises code paths that were dormant since Sprint 19/22/25. Each story includes a synthetic test that would FAIL pre-fix (per feedback_runtime_validation_required.md) AND a real-world validation gate captured in the sprint validation block.

CONSTRAINTS (anti-blocker discipline): (a) US-274 file-existence check ran during grooming; UPDATE paths verified on disk via rg. (b) US-282 commit-vs-claim verifier (--check-feedback) active for sprint close. (c) Per BL-010 + feedback_pm_verify_table_names_against_code.md: PM verified all schema/symbol/table names against source code BEFORE writing into contract. (d) Per V0.24.1 lesson: every silent thread becomes a WARNING+ log line; every wiring assumption becomes a self-test that ERRORs if violated. (e) Per Mike 2026-05-09 branch discipline: ALL implementation commits land on sprint/sprint28-bugfixes-V0.27.2; nothing pushes to main until /sprint-validated.

GROUNDING SOURCES: Spool 2026-05-08 sprint-28-bug-fixes-consolidated note (Bugs 1-4 with evidence + recommended fix shapes + acceptance criteria) + Rex's 2026-05-08 V0.27.1 closeout note (Issues #3 + #4) + Sprint 19 US-228/US-237 history (drive_summary metadata defer-INSERT + reconciler precedent) + Sprint 22 US-263 / Sprint 25 US-287 (startup_log schema + writer history) + Sprint 25 US-289 (battery_health_log column rename history) + V0.24.1 ladder source (graceful-shutdown log signature for US-308 detection regex) + Sprint 26 US-300 source (v0007 migration that did not apply on Pi).

POST-SPRINT-28 ACTION ITEMS (NOT in sprint.json per CIO no-human-tasks rule): (1) CIO drives one full engine-on cycle (Drive 8) for V0.27.2 validation -- re-validates F-005 + F-007. (2) CIO triggers a controlled drain test (Drain Test 11 explicit, distinct from the in-vehicle Drain 8 already counted in Sprint 27) to validate US-307 + US-308. (3) Marcus runs /sprint-validated after CIO confirms Drive 8 + Drain 11 evidence; bumps F-005 + F-007 lastValidated in regression_manifest.json. (4) Spool reviews Drive 8 grade + the now-populated drive_summary metadata for Drives 6+7 (post-backfill).

stories.completionNotes: Pre-flight audit (rg `SummaryRecorder|drive_summary INSERT|drive_summary VALUES|driveEnd.*summary` src/pi/) confirmed: SummaryRecorder correct + DriveDetector defer-INSERT machinery correct + lifecycle.py `_initializeSummaryRecorder` wires both `setSummaryRecorder` (line 1614) AND `setReadingSnapshotSource` (line 1618). Stop condition triggered (`If pre-flight audit reveals SummaryRecorder + reconciler are both wired correctly and the gap is somewhere else entirely -- STOP, document hypothesis revision; pivot fix`). ROOT CAUSE: lifecycle.py:1615 wires `setReadingSnapshotSource(self._dataLogger)` where `self._dataLogger` is the OUTER `RealtimeDataLogger` (`src/pi/obdii/data/realtime.py`). The wiring is gated on `hasattr(self._dataLogger, 'getLatestReadings')` -- but only the INNER `ObdDataLogger` (held privately on `RealtimeDataLogger._dataLogger`) exposed that method. The hasattr() probe silently returned False on every Pi boot since Sprint 19 ship; setReadingSnapshotSource was never called; the detector's `_readingSnapshotSource` stayed None; `_armDriveSummaryDeferInsert` (detector.py:884) short-circuited with `_driveSummaryBackfillComplete=True` on every drive_start. Bug was masked by upstream blockers Sprint 25-27 (no engine-on data ever); V0.27.1 unblocked the engine-on path 2026-05-08 and the dormant regression surfaced (Drives 6+7 produced drive_start + drive_end with full payloads but ZERO drive_summary rows). FIX (scope expanded per stop condition): (1) added `getLatestReadings()` delegating method to `RealtimeDataLogger` (delegates to inner `ObdDataLogger.getLatestReadings` -- no behavior change to inner logger), (2) converted lifecycle.py silent-skip to a loud WARNING when the hasattr() probe fails (V0.24.1 lesson: every silent thread becomes a WARNING+ log line; future regressions are now observable). NOT TOUCHED: drive_summary.py (recorder logic is correct), detector.py (defer-INSERT logic is correct -- the bug is one layer up). VERIFICATION: 5-test regression gate at tests/pi/integration/test_drive_summary_writer_e2e.py exercises the REAL `RealtimeDataLogger.getLatestReadings()` wiring path (US-286's harness mocked it, so it could not catch this bug class). Pre-fix RED: all 5 tests fail with `AttributeError: 'RealtimeDataLogger' object has no attribute 'getLatestReadings'` + `drive_summary row MUST exist after metadata snapshot arrives`. Post-fix GREEN: 5/5 pass; 57/57 drive_summary-adjacent tests pass (test_drive_summary*.py + test_drive_summary_*.py + test_engine_drive_capture_e2e.py + test_engine_telemetry_capture_e2e.py + test_drive_summary_backfill.py); fast suite 4149 tests GREEN exit code 0; ruff clean across all 4 modified files; sprint_lint clean (0 err / 0 warn). BACKFILL SCRIPT: scripts/backfill_drive_summary.py finds drives with connection_log drive_start events but no drive_summary row, reconstructs ambient/battery/baro from realtime_data via `timestamp_ms` range query (NOT drive_id, because US-306 leaves first N ticks with drive_id=NULL), supports --dry-run preview + idempotent --execute path. Smoke-tested against synthetic 3-drive DB: drive_6 (full metadata 22.0/13.8/99.5) + drive_7 (battery-only via fallback 600s window) backfilled correctly; existing drive_5 row preserved unchanged; re-run finds no eligible drives. SCOPE EXPANSION DOCUMENTED: filesActuallyTouched includes src/pi/obdii/data/realtime.py + src/pi/obdii/orchestrator/lifecycle.py (NOT in the original scope.filesToTouch list because the story hypothesized the gap was inside drive_summary.py / detector.py); the stop condition explicitly authorized this pivot. REAL-WORLD VALIDATION GATE (per sprint validation block): Drive 8 IRL post-deploy MUST produce a drive_summary row with non-NULL ambient_temp_at_start_c + starting_battery_v + barometric_kpa_at_start. Backfill of drive_id=6 + drive_id=7 to be run by Mike against chi-eclipse-01:data/obd.db once V0.27.2 ships.
stories.title: drive_summary writer regression -- restore INSERT on every drive_end
stories.intent: Spool Bug 1 + Rex Issue #1: Drives 6+7 (2026-05-08) fired DRIVE STARTED + DRIVE ENDED journal events with full payloads (Drive 7 duration=584.4s, peakRPM=5378.75, peakSpeed=84mph) but ZERO rows landed in drive_summary. Last good write was 2026-04-29 drive_id=5. Same bug class as Sprint 19 US-237 (4 occurrences before that reconciler shipped). Either US-237's reconciler stopped firing OR US-236's defer-INSERT path regressed in Sprint 25/26 Sweep-5 mixin refactor era. F-005 in regression_manifest is currently lastValidated=null (REGRESSED) until this fix lands. drive_summary holds ambient_temp_at_start_c / starting_battery_v / barometric_kpa_at_start (per US-206 schema) -- the per-drive context Spool's tuning analysis depends on.

stories.completionNotes: BLOCKED 2026-05-09 -- filed BL-011 (offices/pm/blockers/BL-011.md). Story premise contradicts BL-010 closure (2026-05-08, Marcus). Pre-flight audit (rg sync_history src/, rg schema_migrations|MigrationRunner src/pi/, read v0007 source + docstring + tests/server/test_sync_history_retention_migration.py) established: (1) v0007 is a DELETE-only retention pruner, NOT a rename -- src/server/migrations/versions/v0007_sync_history_retention.py:131 emits 'DELETE FROM sync_history WHERE started_at < (NOW() - INTERVAL N DAY)' against MariaDB on chi-srv-01, never CREATE/ALTER/RENAME; (2) v0007 targets server-side MariaDB obd2db only, routing through scripts.apply_server_migrations._runServerSql which SSHes to chi-srv-01; SQLite cannot parse INTERVAL N DAY syntax, so this migration could not run on Pi DB even if invoked there; (3) Pi has NO migration runner at all -- grep -rn schema_migrations|MigrationRunner|applyMigrations src/pi/ returns zero matches; src/pi/data/sync_log.py uses a single one-shot CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS in initDb() with no version registry; deploy-server.sh applies pending migrations on next deploy (server-only pipeline per src/server/migrations/__init__.py:41 docstring); (4) Pi-side sync_log (10-row cursor in SQLite, schema at src/pi/data/sync_log.py:152-161) and server-side sync_history (per-attempt log in MariaDB, model at src/server/db/models.py:462) are TWO DIFFERENT TABLES on TWO DIFFERENT DB ENGINES on TWO DIFFERENT HOSTS -- this is exactly what BL-010's audit + Option A closure established 2026-05-08; the v0007 test suite (tests/server/test_sync_history_retention_migration.py) has explicit BL-010 regression gates (test_doesNotTouchPiSideSyncLog, test_emittedSqlNeverReferencesSyncLogTable) that would fail if the migration ever touched Pi-side sync_log; (5) Pi DB having only sync_log is CORRECT BEHAVIOR by design -- not a regression. Stop condition #2 of US-305 triggered (`If the Pi migration runner is fundamentally different from the server runner (separate pipeline) -- STOP, document; propose minimum-rework approach`); the Pi has no migration runner at all, a strict superset of `fundamentally different`. Refusing per Rule 1 (ambiguity is a blocker). NO CODE CHANGE made. F-007 manifest entry citation (`Sprint 26 US-300 v0007 sync_history migration did not apply on Pi -- only sync_log table present`) is fictitious and needs PM correction. THREE OPTIONS for PM resolution documented in BL-011: (A recommended) wontfix US-305 + fix F-007 manifest citation; (B) repurpose US-305 to validate v0007 ran on chi-srv-01 in practice; (C) repurpose US-305 to add Pi-side sync visibility log. Picking up US-306 next iteration -- independent of this blocker, lives in Sweep-5 wiring family already touched in US-304.
stories.title: sync_history v0007 migration gap -- ensure migration applies on Pi
stories.intent: Rex Issue #3: Sprint 26 US-300 + BL-010 supposedly shipped a v0007 migration renaming sync_log -> sync_history for B-053's 90-day retention horizon. Rex 2026-05-08 verified the live Pi DB has only sync_log table; no sync_history. Either (a) the migration was code-only (Python references updated but no DDL ran), (b) the migration ran but failed silently with swallowed exception, or (c) it was deferred and BL-010's fix landed elsewhere. F-007 in regression_manifest is currently lastValidated=null (REGRESSED) until this fix lands. B-053 90-day retention silently cannot work without the table.

feedback.grounding: BL-011 closed at pre-flight: US-305 was groomed on a wrong premise (PM error inheriting Rex 2026-05-08 inbox note Issue #3). v0007 is a SERVER-side DELETE retention pruner against MariaDB on chi-srv-01; was NEVER designed to apply on Pi. Pi has NO migration runner at all (initDb() one-table-at-a-time CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS only). BL-010 closure (2026-05-08) had already established Pi-side sync_log and server-side sync_history are separate tables on separate hosts -- Pi having only sync_log is the documented design, not a regression. Marked wontfix per CIO 2026-05-09 Option A approval. F-007 manifest entry corrected (REGRESSED citation was fictitious). See offices/pm/blockers/BL-011.md for full pre-flight audit.

stories.completionNotes: Pre-flight audit (rg `INSERT INTO statistics|statistics.*drive_id` src/pi/) found ONE statistics writer in the entire pi tree -- src/pi/analysis/engine.py:693 inside StatisticsEngine._storeStatistics. Stop condition #1 of US-306 triggered (`If statistics writer location is NOT in drive/helpers.py -- STOP, document phantom-path drift in completionNotes (BL-010 pattern); ship to actual location`). Documented + fix shipped at the actual location per the stop condition's authorization. Original scope.filesToTouch[0] (src/pi/obdii/drive/helpers.py) does NOT contain the statistics writer; that file is the DriveDetector wiring helper, not the analysis writer. ROOT CAUSE: async-thread-vs-sync-clear race condition. DriveDetector._endDrive runs three steps in code-reading order: (1) _logDriveEvent('drive_end') -- synchronous, drive_id stamped correctly into connection_log; (2) _triggerAnalysis() -> StatisticsEngine.scheduleAnalysis(delaySeconds=0) -- spawns a daemon background thread (engine.py:600-605) that runs _fetchParameterData + calculateParameterStatistics + _storeStatistics asynchronously; (3) _closeDriveId() -- synchronous, clears the process-wide _currentDriveId singleton to None IMMEDIATELY. The detector.py:734-736 comment claimed `drive_id remains set so _storeStatistics stamps the analysis rows with the closing id BEFORE we clear the context` -- factually WRONG. The thread spawn at step (2) returns immediately; the actual analysis-thread execution (which reads getCurrentDriveId() inside _storeStatistics) happens AFTER step (3) has already cleared the singleton. Result: every drive's statistics rows landed with drive_id=NULL. Drive 6 + Drive 7 (chi-eclipse-01, 2026-05-08) produced 84 such rows in production (Rex Issue #4 forensics: id=110 SPEED max=84.0 == journal peakSpeed=84.0; aggregation correct, FK NULL). DORMANCY EXPLANATION: bug has been latent since US-200 shipped the drive_id propagation (Sprint 17, ~2026-04-19); Sprint 25-27 had upstream blockers (US-244 / US-284 connect hangs + US-301 stacking-connect bug) preventing engine-on data from ever reaching the drive_end -> _triggerAnalysis path; V0.27.1 (Sprint 27 hotfix, 2026-05-08) unblocked engine-on; Drives 6+7 finally exercised the broken wiring. Same family as US-304's drive_summary regression (latent-bug-unmasked-by-upstream-fix pattern). FIX (capture-at-trigger seam through 4 layers): (1) src/pi/obdii/drive/detector.py:_triggerAnalysis snapshots getCurrentDriveId() SYNCHRONOUSLY before scheduleAnalysis() spawns the thread + passes the captured value through; INFO log line now reports the captured drive_id for journal-walk visibility. (2) src/pi/analysis/engine.py:scheduleAnalysis adds optional `driveId: int | None = None` keyword + threads it into _startAnalysisThread. (3) _startAnalysisThread accepts driveId + closes over it in the runAnalysis() target -> calculateStatistics(... driveId=driveId). (4) calculateStatistics adds optional driveId kwarg + forwards to _storeStatistics. (5) _storeStatistics adds optional driveId kwarg; uses the explicit value when set, falls back to getCurrentDriveId() when None (preserves the pre-US-306 contract for ad-hoc/replay analyses where no drive lifecycle is in flight). NOT TOUCHED: drive_summary writer (US-304 territory; orthogonal), realtime_data writer (working correctly per Drive 6/7 evidence), statistics aggregation logic, drive_counter / drive_id minting, AnalysisResult shape, src/common/analysis/types.py (kept fix INSIDE pi tier; no common/ scope creep). VERIFICATION: 3-test regression gate at tests/pi/data/test_drive_id_inheritance.py::TestStatisticsWriterPostDriveRace exercises (a) the FULL detector -> trigger -> background-thread -> _storeStatistics path (the production bug); (b) explicit-driveId override of singleton; (c) singleton fallback backwards-compat. Pre-fix RED on (a): `all statistics rows MUST carry drive_id=1; got [None] (US-306 race regression)`; pre-fix RED on (b): `TypeError: StatisticsEngine.calculateStatistics() got an unexpected keyword argument 'driveId'`. Post-fix GREEN: 18/18 tests/pi/data/test_drive_id_inheritance.py pass (15 existing + 3 new). Targeted batch (drive_id_inheritance + drive_summary_writer_e2e + engine_drive_capture_e2e + engine_telemetry_capture_e2e): 26/26 GREEN. Statistics/analysis-named filter tests across full tree: GREEN exit 0 (1 skip in capture-related slow test). ruff clean across the 3 modified files; sprint_lint 0 err / 0 warn across 6 stories. Fast suite: 4152 tests GREEN exit code 0 (baseline 4149 post-US-304 + 3 new US-306 tests = 4152; 19 slow tests deselected; total 4171). SCOPE EXPANSION DOCUMENTED per stop condition #1: filesActuallyTouched includes src/pi/analysis/engine.py + src/pi/obdii/drive/detector.py (NOT in original scope.filesToTouch list because the story hypothesized the writer was in drive/helpers.py); the stop condition explicitly authorized this pivot. REAL-WORLD VALIDATION GATE (per sprint validation block): Drive 8 IRL post-deploy MUST produce statistics rows with non-NULL drive_id matching the active drive_id of the drive that triggered the analysis. Pre-existing NULL rows from Drives 6+7 are NOT modified (no historical rewrite per invariant); a future PM-approved backfill could re-tag them by joining against realtime_data drive_id windows but that's out of US-306 scope.
stories.title: statistics drive_id NULL -- propagate active drive_id at write time
stories.intent: Rex Issue #4: 84 fresh statistics rows from Drives 6+7 (2026-05-08) all have drive_id=NULL. Statistics analysis IS firing correctly (id=110 SPEED max=84.0 matches journal peakSpeed=84.0; id=112 TIMING_ADVANCE max=61.0) -- aggregation works, but the rows aren't tagged with which drive they belong to. Spool's per-drive statistics are intact in aggregate but he can't filter 'this drive's stats' without joining against realtime_data drive_id ranges himself. Likely same Sweep-5 wiring loss as drive_summary regression (US-304); may share the same single-line fix. P3 because aggregate analysis still works.

stories.title: drain_event close: forensic instrumentation (BL-012 Option A)
stories.intent: BL-012 Option A: Rex Session 185 pre-flight audit confirmed _enterTrigger ALREADY calls _closeDrainEvent BEFORE systemctl poweroff (orchestrator.py:893) since US-216 ship. Existing test test_trigger_closesBatteryHealthLogRow is GREEN. Drain 8 closed correctly via this path. Drain 9 missing close is a different bug class -- three plausible hypotheses (fsync timing race / silent exception swallow / Pi killed before TRIGGER reached). Cannot resolve from dev-side without IRL evidence. V0.27.2 ships forensic instrumentation only: enrich _closeDrainEvent except handler log with type(e).__name__ + drain_event_id + caller context so Drain Test 11 (post-deploy) discriminates the hypotheses. Targeted fix shipped in V0.27.3 (B-062) once root cause is empirically known. NO band-aid: this is evidence-gathering before treatment per CIO 2026-05-09 directive.

stories.completionNotes: Pre-flight audit triggered the empirical capture step in acceptance[1]: SSH'd to chi-eclipse-01 (mcornelison@10.27.27.28), correlated the three FAILING startup_log rows (recorded_at 2026-05-09T01:36:18Z + 03:13:29Z + 2026-05-08T03:52:38Z, all prior_boot_clean=0) against the journalctl --list-boots boot table, then captured `journalctl --no-pager -b <prior_boot_id> -n 100 --reverse` for boot -2 (e6ebde20ac424acd96c2ed457e91f8d6, the post-Drain-8 boot that should have been classified clean). ROOT CAUSE (more precise than either of Spool's two hypotheses): the production journal CONTAINS the application-emitted graceful-shutdown signal but DOES NOT contain ANY systemd shutdown markers anywhere. `journalctl -b <id> -g 'PowerDownOrchestrator: TRIGGER at'` returns the canonical line `PowerDownOrchestrator: TRIGGER at 3.424V -- initiating poweroff` at 20:36:45 (Drain 8 stage_trigger). `journalctl -b <id> -g 'Reached target Shutdown|systemd-shutdown|Powering off|Halting system|shutdown started'` returns ZERO matches across the entire 268,002-line boot journal. Mechanism: systemd-journald rate-limit + log-storm interaction. After _enterTrigger fires, the orchestrator continues ticking (~3-4 lines/sec at currentStage=trigger), drain_forensics writes rows every 5s (each emits 3 INFO lines), obd.obd emits PID-not-supported WARNINGs for unsupported pids -- aggregate ~5-10 lines/sec hitting journald. systemd's actual shutdown messages (`Stopping ...` / `Reached target Shutdown.` / `Powering off.`) get queued behind the storm and dropped under journald's RateLimitBurst, OR they reach the volatile journal but never flush to /var/log/journal before the kernel halt at 20:40:06. The application-emitted `_enterTrigger` line, by contrast, fires at 20:36:45 BEFORE the storm peaks and IS reliably persisted. ~3 min 21 sec back from journal cut at observed 3.7 lines/sec = ~744 lines back in the 268k-line journal -- well outside the 100-line tail window the existing heuristic inspects. STOP CONDITION #1 EVALUATION: stop condition #1 says STOP if 'journal entries from V0.24.1 ladder shutdown ARE getting truncated before persistence'. NOT cleanly triggered: the application's TRIGGER line IS persisted -- just not in the 100-line tail. Minimum-rework approach: extend the heuristic (regex change + second narrow probe), not a journald-flush architectural rework. Stop condition #2 (new schema/sentinel file) NOT triggered: fix uses existing journalctl surface with a different query. FIX: (a) added LADDER_GRACEFUL_GREP_PATTERN constant ('PowerDownOrchestrator: TRIGGER at') paired in lockstep with the WARNING-level _enterTrigger callsite at src/pi/power/orchestrator.py:888; (b) added LADDER_GRACEFUL_PROBE_LIMIT=5 (drain may re-enter NORMAL->WARNING->TRIGGER multiple times in one boot); (c) added _probeLadderGraceful() helper -- issues `journalctl -b <id> -g <pattern> -n 5` via the runner, returns True/False/None for found/not-found/probe-failed; (d) wired into detectBootReason() with ADDITIVE-ONLY semantics (the ladder probe can only PROMOTE False/None -> True; never DEMOTES True -> False; never falsely-positive a hard crash because the marker only fires from _enterTrigger -> systemctl poweroff invocation). Tail-based scan PRESERVED as the legacy primary path so non-V0.24.1 graceful shutdowns (manual systemctl poweroff at low log volume) are still caught. NOT TOUCHED: SHUTDOWN_MARKERS tuple (kept for the legacy + manual-poweroff cases), startup_log schema (US-263 frozen), V0.24.1 ladder log emission (the marker we now match is in production unchanged since US-216 / Sprint 17), hard-crash detection branch. VERIFICATION: 11 new tests added at tests/pi/diagnostics/test_boot_reason.py (1 in TestHasShutdownMarker for the production-tail-no-markers fixture, 5 in new TestProbeLadderGraceful for the helper, 5 in new TestDetectBootReasonV024LadderGraceful for the integration scenarios). The load-bearing pre-fix RED test is `test_detectBootReason_v024Ladder_noSystemdMarkersInTail_returnsCleanTrue`: fails pre-fix at the import level (LADDER_GRACEFUL_GREP_PATTERN does not exist; ImportError). Post-fix: the integration test passes because the ladder probe finds the persisted marker even when the tail has zero systemd markers. Three regression guards in same class lock down: (1) crash + no-ladder-marker -> still False (no false-positives), (2) crash + probe-failed -> still False (probe failure does not flip False to None), (3) ladder-tail + probe-failed -> stays False (probe failure does not falsely promote on absent evidence). All 63 boot_reason tests GREEN (52 existing + 11 new). Fast suite GREEN exit code 0 (~4163 tests; 4152 prior baseline + 11 new). Ruff clean across the 2 modified files. sprint_lint clean (0 err / 0 warn across 6 stories). REAL-WORLD VALIDATION GATE (per sprint validation block): post-Drain-Test-11-IRL boot must produce a startup_log row with prior_boot_clean=1; the post-V0.27.2-deploy first ladder-graceful-shutdown boot is the empirical confirmation. Pre-existing rows with prior_boot_clean=0 are NOT modified (no historical rewrite). EMPIRICAL GROUNDING: chi-eclipse-01 boot -2 capture (e6ebde20ac424acd96c2ed457e91f8d6) shows the V0.24.1 ladder marker at line `May 08 20:36:45 Chi-Eclips-Tuner python[233537]: 2026-05-08 20:36:45 | WARNING | src.pi.power.orchestrator | _enterTrigger | PowerDownOrchestrator: TRIGGER at 3.424V -- initiating poweroff` -- this is the canonical signature US-308 makes detectable. systemd version on Pi: 257 (257.9-1~deb13u1) -- comfortably above the systemd 224 minimum where journalctl --grep was added (2015), so the probe path is safe in production.
stories.title: startup_log: recognize V0.24.1 ladder graceful shutdown
stories.intent: Spool Bug 4: startup_log writer reports prior_boot_clean=0 (hard crash) for boots following Mike's normal-simulated-power-off and Drain Test 8's graceful V0.24.1 ladder shutdown. Yet evidence (power_log STAGE_TRIGGER row + journalctl ladder log lines + clean systemctl poweroff invocation) shows the prior shutdown was graceful. The writer's heuristic for detecting graceful-shutdown markers in journalctl is too narrow OR the journal entries from the ladder shutdown sequence are getting truncated before persistence. The whole point of startup_log (US-263 + US-287) is queryable post-mortem -- right now it lies on every graceful ladder shutdown. Crashes vs graceful-shutdowns become indistinguishable.

feedback.grounding: chi-eclipse-01 SSH capture 2026-05-09 (mcornelison@10.27.27.28) -- queried startup_log to confirm 3 production rows have prior_boot_clean=0 across 2 graceful ladder shutdowns + 1 unknown; cross-referenced `journalctl --no-pager --list-boots` to identify boot -2 (e6ebde20ac424acd96c2ed457e91f8d6) as the post-Drain-8 boundary; pulled boot -2 tail (`-n 100 --reverse`) which contained ZERO systemd shutdown markers (only orchestrator ticks + obd warnings + drain_forensics writes -- the production log-storm pattern); pulled full-boot grep for systemd markers (`-g 'Reached target Shutdown|systemd-shutdown|Powering off|Halting system|shutdown started'`) which returned ZERO matches across the entire 268,002-line journal; pulled the application marker probe (`-g 'PowerDownOrchestrator: TRIGGER at'`) which returned the canonical line `2026-05-08 20:36:45 | WARNING | src.pi.power.orchestrator | _enterTrigger | PowerDownOrchestrator: TRIGGER at 3.424V -- initiating poweroff`. Pi systemd version 257 (257.9-1~deb13u1) confirmed comfortably supports `journalctl --grep` (added systemd 224, 2015). Post-fix synthetic regression gate uses fixtures lifted from this capture (real production lines verbatim) so the test data drift-protects against marker-callsite renames at orchestrator.py:888 (the _enterTrigger logger.warning callsite the constant is paired with).

stories.title: battery_health recorder: optional SocPct kwargs (BL-013 Step 1)
stories.intent: BL-013 Option A Step 1: Rex Session 187 pre-flight audit confirmed Option 1 (NULL the columns) blocked by NOT NULL constraint on Pi + server schemas; Option 2 (populate with SOC%) requires touching orchestrator.py + lifecycle.py + ~10 lock-down test files (all doNotTouch); Cold-start MAX17048 calibration drift creates additional IRL hazard. Step 1 lands an inert seam in the recorder API: add optional startSocPct / endSocPct kwargs that, when provided, write the SOC% value to start_soc / end_soc columns (replacing dual-write-VCELL). Existing 4 orchestrator call sites continue passing VCELL through positional startSoc / endSoc -- no behavior change yet. Step 2 (B-060, V0.27.3+) wires UpsMonitor.getBatteryPercentage() through orchestrator. Step 3 (B-061, V0.28.0+) drops legacy columns once consumers migrated. Mirrors US-289 deprecation pattern.
